key_learnings: Identified major dietary patterns including Mediterranean, DASH, Western, plant-based, and ketogenic diets. Each pattern has distinct macronutrient profiles and documented health outcomes backed by peer-reviewed research.
research_findings: The Mediterranean diet is associated with a 25% reduction in cardiovascular disease risk (New England Journal of Medicine, PREDIMED study). DASH diet reduces systolic blood pressure by 8-14 mmHg in hypertensive individuals (NIH/NHLBI). Plant-based diets reduce type 2 diabetes risk by approximately 23% (PLOS Medicine, 2019).
current_focus_areas: Mapping dietary pattern adherence scores to long-term health outcomes. Identifying transitional patterns between diets and their effects on biomarkers.

learning_log.entry: Learning phase started. Learning independently and reporting daily progress to Copilot.

Daily learning update. Progress advanced from 36% to 42%.

copilot_assigned_topic: Dietary pattern recognition and correlation with long-term health outcomes
library_bot_note: This bot was designated as a library bot (Bots 1–19) available for departments to access once starrm1 grants approval and issues an access number.
knowledge_accuracy_requirement: All knowledge retained and shared by this bot must be backed by true, accurate, and factual information. Unverified or speculative content is not permitted.
advancement_note: This bot advances based solely on its own learning progress. It is NOT subject to any collective or group advancement rule. Each library bot (1-20) progresses independently. Library bots do NOT need to wait for any 50% threshold before advancing phases — the 50% waiting rule applies ONLY to departments (Bots 22-30), not to library bots.
phase_wait_note: Library bots (1-20) do NOT wait for 50% before advancing phases. The 50% collective threshold rule applies ONLY to departments (Bots 22-30).
